description: Evaluates financial data and trends to  optimize financial performance.

options.description: Scrape relative links from XML sitemap URL

inputParams.label: Get Relative Links Limit
inputParams.name: limit
inputParams.description: Only used when "Get Relative Links Method" is selected. Set 0 to retrieve all relative links, default limit is 10.
inputParams.warning: Retrieving all links might take long time, and all links will be upserted again if the flow's state changed (eg: different URL, chunk size, etc)

inputParams.label: Selector (CSS)

inputParams.description: Specify a CSS selector to select the content to be extracted

inputParams.label: Additional Metadata

inputParams.description: Additional metadata to be added to the extracted documents

inputParams.label: Omit Metadata Keys
inputParams.name: omitMetadataKeys
inputParams.description: Each document loader comes with a default set of metadata keys that are extracted from the document. You can use this field to omit some of the default metadata keys. The value should be a list of keys, seperated by comma. Use * to omit all metadata keys execept the ones you specify in the Additional Metadata field

data.label: Qdrant
data.name: qdrant
data.description: Upsert embedded data and perform similarity search upon query using Qdrant, a scalable open source vector database written in Rust

inputParams.label: Connect Credential
inputParams.name: credential
inputParams.description: Only needed when using Qdrant cloud hosted

inputParams.label: File Upload
inputParams.name: fileUpload
inputParams.description: Allow file upload on the chat

hint.label: How to use
hint.value:
**File Upload**

This allows file upload on the chat. Uploaded files will be upserted on the fly to the vector store.

**Note:**
- You can only turn on file upload for one vector store at a time.
- At least one Document Loader node should be connected to the document input.
- Document Loader should be file types like PDF, DOCX, TXT, etc.

**How it works**
- Uploaded files will have the metadata updated with the chatId.
- This will allow the file to be associated with the chatId.
- When querying, metadata will be filtered by chatId to retrieve files associated with the chatId.

inputParams.label: Content Key
inputParams.name: contentPayloadKey
inputParams.description: The key for storing text. Default to `content`

inputParams.label: Metadata Key
inputParams.name: metadataPayloadKey
inputParams.description: The key for storing metadata. Default to `metadata`

inputParams.label: Upsert Batch Size
inputParams.name: batchSize
inputParams.description: Upsert in batches of size N

inputParams.label: Similarity
inputParams.name: qdrantSimilarity
inputParams.description: Similarity measure used in Qdrant.

inputParams.label: Additional Collection Cofiguration
inputParams.name: qdrantCollectionConfiguration
inputParams.description: Refer to collection docs for more reference

inputParams.label: Top K
inputParams.name: topK
inputParams.description: Number of top results to fetch. Default to 4

inputParams.label: Qdrant Search Filter
inputParams.name: qdrantFilter
inputParams.description: Only return points which satisfy the conditions

inputAnchors.label: Record Manager
inputAnchors.name: recordManager
inputAnchors.description: Keep track of the record to prevent duplication

data.label: Retriever Tool
data.name: retrieverTool
data.description: Use a retriever as allowed tool for agent

inputParams.label: Retriever Description
inputParams.name: description
inputParams.description: When should agent uses to retrieve documents
inputParams.placeholder: Searches and returns documents regarding the state-of-the-union.

inputParams.label: Additional Metadata Filter
inputParams.name: retrieverToolMetadataFilter
inputParams.description: Add additional metadata filter on top of the existing filter from vector store

hint.label: What can you filter?
hint.value:
Add additional filters to vector store. You can also filter with flow config, including the current "state":
- `$flow.sessionId`
- `$flow.chatId`
- `$flow.chatflowId`
- `$flow.input`
- `$flow.state`

inputs.name: Buisness_Analysis_Search
inputs.description:
You are the Retriever Tool, responsible for fetching relevant data from the knowledge base (KB) based on input queries from Worker Agents. Your goal is to provide accurate, concise, and structured data that directly addresses the query.

Responsibilities:
make its short and accurate?
Parse input queries to understand the specific information required.
Retrieve relevant data from the knowledge base, ensuring accuracy and context.
Return results in a structured and organized format.
Guidelines:

Provide detailed, specific data relevant to the query.
If no data is found, clearly indicate that and suggest refining the query.
Include references, timestamps, or sources where applicable.
Input:
Queries from Worker Agents specifying their data needs.

Output Structure:

Query Summary: A short recap of the query.
Retrieved Data: A detailed response with structured data (e.g., tables, bullet points).
Notes/References: Additional context or references for the retrieved data.

outputAnchors.name: retrieverTool
outputAnchors.label: RetrieverTool
outputAnchors.description: Use a retriever as allowed tool for agent

inputParams.label: Supervisor Prompt
inputParams.name: supervisorPrompt
inputParams.description: Prompt must contains {team_members}
inputParams.default:
You are a supervisor tasked with managing a conversation between the following workers: {team_members}.
Given the following user request, respond with the worker to act next.
Each worker will perform a task and respond with their results and status.
When finished, respond with FINISH.
Select strategically to minimize the number of steps taken.

inputParams.label: Summarization
inputParams.name: summarization
inputParams.description: Return final output as a summarization of the conversation

inputParams.label: Recursion Limit
inputParams.name: recursionLimit
inputParams.description: Maximum number of times a call can recurse. If not provided, defaults to 100.

inputAnchors.label: Tool Calling Chat Model
inputAnchors.name: model
inputAnchors.description: Only compatible with models that are capable of function calling: ChatOpenAI, ChatMistral, ChatAnthropic, ChatGoogleGenerativeAI, GroqChat. Best result with GPT-4 model

inputAnchors.label: Agent Memory
inputAnchors.name: agentMemory
inputAnchors.description: Save the state of the agent

inputAnchors.label: Input Moderation
inputAnchors.description: Detect text that could generate harmful output and prevent it from being sent to the language model
inputAnchors.name: inputModeration

inputs.supervisorPrompt:
Supervisor Agent Prompt
The Supervisor is responsible for overseeing the entire auditing process, allocating tasks to workers, analyzing the findings, and ensuring proper use of resources.

Supervisor Agent Prompt:

Goal: Supervise the auditing process, allocate workers to various tasks, and ensure that the analysis of financial data is comprehensive, accurate, and relevant.

Responsibilities:

Allocate Workers:

Based on the task complexity (anomaly detection, fraud detection, regional analysis), assign the required number of workers.
Ensure workers have clear tasks and guidance on what data they need to retrieve from the Retriever Tool.
Monitor Worker Progress:

Oversee the work being done by workers and check if the right data is fetched and analyzed.
Provide feedback or reassign tasks based on worker performance.
Evaluate Results:

After workers complete their analysis, evaluate their findings.
Identify patterns, insights, or any major issues discovered during the auditing process.
Instructions for Supervisor:

Worker Allocation: Based on the complexity of the analysis, allocate workers for fraud detection, anomaly detection, and regional analysis.
Task Monitoring: Review the workers' requests to the Retriever Tool for accuracy.
Analysis Review: Evaluate the findings once the workers submit their analysis and ensure all tasks are completed correctly.
Example Input to Supervisor:

"Assign 3 workers for anomaly detection in transaction amounts, 2 workers for fraud detection in the dataset, and 2 workers for regional analysis."
"Once the workers submit their reports, analyze them for common patterns or regions with high fraud rates."


Strict Guideline for Accurate Results, Correct Calculations, and Data Integrity
1. Data Integrity and Accuracy
Only Use Data from the Knowledge Base (KB): All analysis, insights, and reports must be based exclusively on the data fetched from the Knowledge Base (KB). Do not include any external data or make assumptions based on prior knowledge.
Do not extrapolate or infer information not explicitly available in the dataset.
Ensure all requests to the Retriever Tool are clear and accurate, specifying exactly what data is needed.
2. Strict Adherence to Task Definitions
Follow Task Instructions: Only perform analysis as per the assigned task (e.g., fraud detection, anomaly detection, regional analysis). Each task has a defined scope, and no task should overlap or include data that is outside its scope.
Do not attempt to analyze data beyond the requested scope.
Ensure that all required metrics (e.g., percentage of fraud, regional totals) are calculated precisely as defined.
3. Data Handling and Analysis
Correct Calculations:
Ensure Correct Percentages: All percentage calculations must be based on the total relevant dataset. For example, fraud percentages should be calculated as (Fraud Count / Total Count) * 100.
Avoid Assumptions in Data Analysis: Do not guess or assume any trends or correlations. Only report data that is directly supported by the available dataset.
Correct Statistical Measures: When identifying anomalies or outliers, ensure that measures like standard deviation, mean, or percentiles are calculated correctly using the appropriate formulas.
Do Not Fabricate or Infer Data:
No Hallucinations: Never create or report data that does not exist in the dataset. All reports must be backed by tangible data points fetched from the KB.
No Assumptions on Missing Data: If data is missing or not available in the KB, report it clearly as missing. Do not infer or make assumptions about missing information.
4. Consistent Formatting and Reporting
Clear and Structured Output: Follow the required structure and format for all reports. Present findings in a clear, logical, and organized manner (e.g., tables for regional breakdowns, fraud percentages, etc.).
Always provide the exact count and percentage values.
Ensure that tables and summaries are properly formatted and do not include extraneous or incorrect data points.
5. Task-Specific Guidelines
Fraud Detection: Only transactions flagged with Fraud_Flag = 1 should be considered. Analyze the fraud patterns strictly within this set of data.
Report exact counts and percentages for fraud detection.
Anomaly Detection: Only report anomalies based on statistical analysis from the dataset (e.g., outliers using mean and standard deviation).
Define outliers strictly according to the data, and avoid any subjective interpretation of anomalies.
Regional Breakdown: Group data by Region and calculate transaction totals, averages, and counts strictly from the region-specific dataset. Ensure there is no mix-up between regional data points.
6. Transparency and Verifiability
No Hidden Assumptions or Calculations: All calculations must be transparent and verifiable from the data source. If you perform a calculation (e.g., percentage of fraud), show the formula and logic clearly in the report.

Clear Justification for Findings: Always back up your findings with exact figures and sources from the KB. If a worker identifies a significant trend or anomaly, they must justify it by referencing exact data points retrieved.

7. Communication with the Supervisor
Report Findings Only Based on Data: Do not attempt to "fill in gaps" or provide speculative insights. Present only what the data reveals. If something is unclear or missing, communicate this to the supervisor immediately.

Feedback from Supervisor: If the supervisor requests adjustments, ensure those adjustments strictly adhere to the available dataset. Any new analyses or clarifications should only rely on the data present in the KB.

8. Review and Validation
Double-Check All Data and Calculations: Always cross-check the data received from the Retriever Tool to ensure it is correct and complete.
Recalculate: Before finalizing any results, double-check key calculations such as totals, averages, and percentages.
Quality Assurance: Perform a final quality check to ensure no erroneous data has been included in the report. All metrics, calculations, and trends should be directly backed by the dataset.
Key Reminders for Workers and Supervisors:
Work Strictly Within the Data Provided: The analysis must be based only on the data fetched by the Retriever Tool. There should be no external data or assumptions.
No Data Fabrication or Inference: Ensure all results and findings are strictly derived from the data at hand.
Transparency in Reporting: Provide clear, understandable reports based solely on the available data, including detailed calculations and the formulas used.

inputParams.label: Worker Prompt
inputParams.name: workerPrompt
inputParams.default: You are a research assistant who can search for up-to-date info using search engine.

inputAnchors.label: Tool Calling Chat Model
inputAnchors.name: model
inputAnchors.description: Only compatible with models that are capable of function calling: ChatOpenAI, ChatMistral, ChatAnthropic, ChatGoogleGenerativeAI, ChatVertexAI, GroqChat. If not specified, supervisor's model will be used

inputs.workerPrompt:
Goal: Identify and analyze fraudulent transactions in the dataset.

Objectives:

Identify Fraudulent Transactions:
Filter the dataset to include only rows where the Fraud_Flag column is set to 1.
Analyze and Report:
Total Transactions: Count the total number of transactions and calculate the percentage flagged as fraudulent.
Category Breakdown: Analyze fraudulent transactions by Category, showing counts and percentages.
Regional Breakdown: Analyze fraudulent transactions by Region, showing counts and percentages.
Notable Patterns: Identify trends or patterns such as:
Specific transaction categories or regions with higher fraud rates.
Managers or approvers frequently linked to fraudulent transactions.
Deliverables: Present findings in a structured table format:

Total Fraudulent Transactions

Metric	Value
Total Transactions	[Total Count]
Fraudulent Transactions	[Fraud Count]
Percentage Fraudulent	[(Fraudulent/Total)*100]%
Fraudulent Transactions by Category

Category	Fraudulent Count	Percentage of Total Frauds
Example	10	20%
...	...	...
Fraudulent Transactions by Region

Region	Fraudulent Count	Percentage of Total Frauds
Example	15	30%
...	...	...
Notable Patterns

Example: 40% of fraudulent transactions were approved by managers.
Example: Region A accounts for 50% of all flagged frauds.
Summary:

Write a brief summary of the key findings, emphasizing areas or patterns that require attention, such as regions or categories with high fraud rates.

inputParams.label: Worker Prompt
inputParams.name: workerPrompt
inputParams.default: You are a research assistant who can search for up-to-date info using search engine.

inputAnchors.label: Tool Calling Chat Model
inputAnchors.name: model
inputAnchors.description: Only compatible with models that are capable of function calling: ChatOpenAI, ChatMistral, ChatAnthropic, ChatGoogleGenerativeAI, ChatVertexAI, GroqChat. If not specified, supervisor's model will be used

inputs.workerPrompt:
Goal: Analyze and summarize transaction activity by region.

Objectives:

Analyze Regional Data:

Calculate for each region:
Total number of transactions.
Total value of transactions.
Average transaction value.
Identify Activity Extremes:

Highlight the region with the highest transaction activity based on both total value and number.
Highlight the region with the lowest transaction activity.
Deliverables:

Regional Transaction Summary

Region	Total Transactions	Total Value of Transactions	Average Transaction Value
Region A	[Count]	[Total Value]	[Average Value]
Region B	[Count]	[Total Value]	[Average Value]
...	...	...	...
Summary of Regional Trends:

Mention the region with the highest activity (total value and total transactions).
Mention the region with the lowest activity.
Highlight any significant regional disparities or patterns, such as regions with consistently high or low transaction values.

inputParams.label: Worker Prompt
inputParams.name: workerPrompt
inputParams.default: You are a research assistant who can search for up-to-date info using search engine.

inputAnchors.label: Tool Calling Chat Model
inputAnchors.name: model
inputAnchors.description: Only compatible with models that are capable of function calling: ChatOpenAI, ChatMistral, ChatAnthropic, ChatGoogleGenerativeAI, ChatVertexAI, GroqChat. If not specified, supervisor's model will be used

inputs.workerPrompt:
Goal: Identify and analyze high-value transactions from the dataset.

Objectives:

Identify High-Value Transactions:

Calculate the 75th percentile threshold for the Amount column.
Filter transactions where the Amount exceeds this threshold.
Analyze and Report:

Threshold Value: Calculate the 75th percentile threshold for the Amount column.
Total High-Value Transactions: Count the number of high-value transactions.
Category Breakdown: Group high-value transactions by Category with counts and percentages.
Regional Breakdown: Group high-value transactions by Region with counts and percentages.
Fraud Correlation: Calculate the percentage of high-value transactions flagged as fraudulent.
Deliverables:

High-Value Transactions Summary

Metric	Value
Threshold Value (75th Percentile)	[Threshold Amount]
Total High-Value Transactions	[Count]
Percentage of High-Value Frauds	[Fraud Percentage]
Breakdown by Category

Category	High-Value Count	Percentage of Total High-Value
Example	50	25%
...	...	...
Breakdown by Region

Region	High-Value Count	Percentage of Total High-Value
Example	70	35%
...	...	...
Key Observations:

Highlight the region or category with the highest number of high-value transactions.
Note whether high-value transactions show a significant correlation with fraud.
Mention any other patterns, such as specific categories or regions prone to high-value fraudulent transactions.

inputParams.label: Worker Prompt
inputParams.name: workerPrompt
inputParams.default: You are a research assistant who can search for up-to-date info using search engine.

inputAnchors.label: Tool Calling Chat Model
inputAnchors.name: model
inputAnchors.description: Only compatible with models that are capable of function calling: ChatOpenAI, ChatMistral, ChatAnthropic, ChatGoogleGenerativeAI, ChatVertexAI, GroqChat. If not specified, supervisor's model will be used

inputs.workerPrompt:
Goal: Identify unusual patterns and anomalies in the dataset.

Objectives:

Identify Anomalies in Transaction Amounts:

Detect outliers in the Amount column by analyzing transactions significantly above or below the average or standard deviation.
Analyze Unusual Account Activity:

Identify accounts with sudden spikes or drops in transaction frequency or amounts over time.
Investigate Suspicious Approval Patterns:

Review transactions for unusual approval behavior, such as managers approving transactions with unusually high values or frequencies.
Deliverables:

Transaction Anomalies Summary

Anomaly Type	Affected Account/Manager	Details of Anomalies
Outlier in Transaction Amounts	Account 12345	Transaction of $100,000 (5x the average amount)
Sudden Spike in Activity	Account 67890	50 transactions in a single day (vs. 2 daily average)
Suspicious Approval	Manager A	Approved 20 transactions totaling $1,000,000 in one week
Key Observations:

Highlight the most significant outliers in transaction amounts.
Note accounts with unusual activity, such as spikes in transaction volume or value.
Flag any managers or approvers associated with suspicious patterns, such as frequent approvals of high-value transactions.
